items.definition_en: Fever is when your body temperature is higher than normal.
items.example_en: He stayed home from school because he had a fever.

items.definition_en: Cough is a sudden, loud sound you make when your throat hurts.
items.example_en: Her cough kept her up all night.

items.definition_en: A cold is an illness with a runny nose and sneezing.
items.example_en: I caught a cold after going out in the rain.

items.definition_en: The flu is a sickness with fever, aches, and tiredness.
items.example_en: The flu made me stay in bed for days.

items.definition_en: Pain is the feeling you have when something hurts.
items.example_en: She felt pain in her arm after the fall.

items.definition_en: An allergy is when your body reacts badly to something.
items.example_en: She takes medicine for her allergy to pollen.

items.definition_en: Asthma is a condition that makes it hard to breathe.
items.example_en: He always carries an inhaler for his asthma.

items.definition_en: A rash is red or sore skin, often from an allergy.
items.example_en: Her skin had a rash after eating strawberries.

items.definition_en: A cut is a place where the skin has been opened by something sharp.
items.example_en: He got a small cut on his finger.

items.definition_en: A bruise is a dark mark on the skin after being hit.
items.example_en: She has a bruise on her knee from falling.

items.definition_en: A burn is an injury caused by something hot.
items.example_en: He showed his mother the burn on his hand.

items.definition_en: An infection is when germs make you sick.
items.example_en: The doctor said he had a chest infection.

items.definition_en: A sore throat is pain or irritation in the throat.
items.example_en: She cannot sing because of her sore throat.

items.definition_en: A headache is a pain in your head.
items.example_en: He took medicine for his headache.

items.definition_en: Toothache is pain in a tooth or teeth.
items.example_en: She couldn't eat because of toothache.

items.definition_en: A stomachache is pain in your stomach.
items.example_en: He got a stomachache after eating too much.

items.definition_en: Diarrhea is a sickness where you need to go to the toilet often.
items.example_en: She stayed home from work because of diarrhea.

items.definition_en: Vomiting is when food and drink come out from your mouth suddenly.
items.example_en: After the ride, he started vomiting.

items.definition_en: An injury is harm or damage to your body.
items.example_en: He missed the game because of an injury.

items.definition_en: Bleeding is when blood is coming out of your body.
items.example_en: She tried to stop the bleeding with a tissue.
